_comment: THE RUIN — Threshold (Rung-5 puzzle world, solo-first slice). An overgrown antechamber built, long ago, to be worked by a person AND their companion together. A heavy stone slab bars the way deeper; its mechanism is a weight-plate buried under the moss that only the companion's search can uncover. See docs/puzzle-world-the-ruin-design.md. Coordinates are world-space pixels; colours are [r,g,b] 0..1. The 'ruin' block (below) describes the pair-operated wards the controller drives via RuinMechanisms; the slab itself is also listed as an interactable so world_art draws it and Solids blocks it. NOTE: the Seek command + the referee that uncovers the plate and raises the slab are the NEXT build step — until then the slab is a real barrier with no opener yet (walk back through the entry portal to leave).
_regions_comment: Named AREAS for new-area discovery (a bond source). 'threshold' is the antechamber you arrive in; 'inner' is the calm nook beyond the slab.
_border_comment: A dense, jittered ring of old trees crowding the ruin's edge, so the place feels enclosed and overgrown rather than ending at the void. Presentation + colliders both come from this (seeded, identical every run).
_collision_comment: Tall props block by default; the slab and the ruined-wall columns set 'solid' explicitly with their own radii. body_radius/margin are the character size + a small standoff.
_atmosphere_comment: Dim, cool, mossy and enclosed. day_tint a cool green wash; a strong vignette closes the walls in; sparse pale motes drift like spores in shafts of light; the ground is mottled moss.
_wall_comment: The ruined cross-wall: a row of fallen columns (solid) spanning the antechamber, with a single doorway at x=0 barred by the slab. Columns are 60px apart so their blocking circles meet — no sneaking between them — and the border ring closes the ends. Drawn as the fallback prop for now; bespoke 'column' art comes with the opener step.

interactables.label: a faint glimmer in the dark

_ruin_comment: The pair-operated WARDS the controller drives via RuinMechanisms. A ward links a hidden weight-plate to a slab: the companion's Seek search uncovers the plate when it passes within uncover_radius, and the slab raises (and, with latch=true, stays raised — the Threshold doorway) once the companion settles its weight within occupy_radius. The plate has NO interactable entry: it's invisible until the search reveals it (the controller spawns its reveal art then). 'hint' is shown when you near the slab with no idea how to open it.

wards.hint: The slab won't budge. Maybe your companion can find what works it — try sending it to look.

_portals_comment: ruin_entry is where you arrive from the Vale (and step back through to leave); ruin_exit, in the inner nook beyond the slab, leads home once you've opened the way.
